title: Queries, Keys, and Values
concept: The Query (what we want), Key (indexing tags), and Value (content vectors) model matches database search: match Query to Key to fetch Value.

exercises.prompt: Which concept uses Queries, Keys, and Values to help AI focus on specific words?
exercises.explain: The Query, Key, and Value model is a core part of the attention mechanism used in modern AI models like Transformers.

cards.title: Finding the Right Connections
cards.body: How does an AI know which words in a sentence relate to each other? It uses a system inspired by database searches, matching requests to indexes to pull the right meanings.

cards.title: Meet Q, K, and V
cards.body: In AI attention, we divide information into three parts: the Query (what information you are looking for), the Key (the labels or tags that describe other words), and the Value (the actual content or meaning of those words).

cards.title: The YouTube Search
cards.body: Think of searching for a video on YouTube. Your search text is the Query. The video titles and tags are the Keys. The actual video content you watch is the Value. You match your Query to the closest Keys to get the best Value!

cards.title: Context Matching
cards.body: When an AI translates 'The bank of the river', it looks at the word 'bank' (Query). It checks neighboring words like 'river' (Keys). Since 'river' matches water concepts better than money, the AI fetches the water-related meaning (Value) of 'bank'.

cards.title: Three Roles, One Word
cards.body: People often think the Query, Key, and Value are the same vector. In reality, they are three different representations of the same input word, created by multiplying the word by three different matrices.

cards.title: Database vs. Attention
cards.body: This comparison shows how database searches and AI attention mechanisms align.

right.text: Create a Query vector, compare it to Key vectors, and combine the Value vectors.

left.text: Enter a Search Term, scan through file Tags, and open the matching Document.

exercises.prompt: What does the 'Query' represent in the attention mechanism?

options.text: The target information or search term we are looking for
The final output category of the entire network
A database table that stores raw training text
A secret password used to encrypt the neural network

exercises.explain: The Query represents what the current word is looking for in other words to understand its own context.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In attention mechanisms, we compare the Query with the Keys to decide how much of each Value to keep.
exercises.explain: The Query is matched against the Keys. The resulting match scores are used to weight and combine the Values.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing part of the database search analogy.
exercises.sentence: In attention, we match the Query to the Key to retrieve the correct ________.
exercises.explain: The Query is what we search for, the Key is the label we compare it against, and the Value is the actual content we retrieve.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes an AI system using the Query, Key, and Value framework to pay attention?

options.text: Matching the user's prompt (Query) against document tags (Keys) to retrieve the relevant paragraphs (Values).
Looking up a word in a static dictionary file by checking alphabetical index tabs.
Generating random sentences and checking if they contain the letters Q, K, and V.

exercises.explain: Option A shows an AI matching search terms (Query) to tags (Keys) to get content (Values). Option B is just a hardcoded list, and Option C is random generation.

exercises.prompt: When an AI model processes a word, how does it use the Query, Key, and Value components to focus its attention?

options.text: It ignores the Keys and directly copies all Values.
It uses Keys to find the Query, then outputs the Key names.
It compares the Query with all Keys to fetch the weighted Values.
It deletes the Values that do not match the Query.

exercises.explain: The attention mechanism uses a Query (search terms) to compare against Keys (file tags) to determine how much attention to pay to each corresponding Value (the actual file contents).
